key_insights.insight: Neurodegenerative diseases are not inevitable and lifestyle factors play a significant role in prevention.
key_insights.quote: These diseases that we look at as inevitable parts of aging, they're just not, right? They are not inevitable. A lot of them are a product of our lifestyle.

key_insights.insight: Even small increases in physical activity, especially for those starting from a sedentary lifestyle, yield significant cardiovascular and brain health benefits.
key_insights.quote: The biggest bang for your buck from a public health standpoint are people who are starting from almost zero and just starting to exercise a little bit... getting four or 5,000 steps per day is going to give you a big cardiovascular benefit and a big benefit for your brain.

key_insights.insight: Combining physical activity with cognitive challenges, such as spatial navigation, can enhance the benefits of exercise on cognitive performance.
key_insights.quote: If you combine physical activity with cognitive challenges, you can boost the effects of physical activity on the brain, on especially cognitive performance.

key_insights.insight: Prolonged sitting is associated with negative health outcomes, and breaking up sitting into smaller periods is beneficial.
key_insights.quote: Breaking up sitting into smaller periods is probably the best thing we can do.

key_insights.insight: Exercising in green spaces or nature seems to provide greater mood-boosting benefits compared to exercising in urban or indoor environments.
key_insights.quote: Moving outside and especially moving in green spaces... seems to have bigger benefits, especially for mood.

key_insights.insight: Low-intensity physical activity, even the kind that requires small shifts in your body is better than sitting still for long periods.
key_insights.quote: They're resting in postures that require a little muscle activity... it's possible that just that kind of low level of muscle activity is how our bodies are well adapted to rest.

key_insights.insight: When you're active, your muscles generate proteins called myokines which travel to the brain and act like fertilizer for brain cells.
key_insights.quote: Another thing that happens is that your muscles actually generate proteins called myokines... those travel to the brain and they interact with neurons in ways that upregulate neurotrophins.

key_insights.insight: Excessive alcohol consumption (over one drink per day) is associated with negative health outcomes, including increased risk of dementia and Alzheimer's disease.
key_insights.quote: Excessive consumption over the sort of one drink per day or two drinks per day for a man, one drink per day for a woman, that level of alcohol consumption is associated with negative health outcomes, including risk of dementia and Alzheimer's disease.

actionable_advice.advice: Incorporate 'exercise snack breaks' every 30-45 minutes during prolonged sitting.
actionable_advice.how_to: Get up and walk up a staircase, do some push-ups, or do some squats.

actionable_advice.advice: Increase daily water intake to force more frequent breaks from sitting.
actionable_advice.how_to: Drink more water throughout the day to increase the need to get up and use the restroom.

actionable_advice.advice: Make walking or running more cognitively stimulating.
actionable_advice.how_to: Vary routes frequently, explore new areas, and use spatial navigation skills to find your way around.

actionable_advice.advice: Incorporate short bursts of vigorous intermittent physical activity into your daily routine.
actionable_advice.how_to: Walk to lunch at a brisk pace, take the stairs instead of the elevator, or walk to destinations whenever possible.

actionable_advice.advice: Make a point to exercise in green spaces (parks, trails) when possible
actionable_advice.how_to: Instead of running on a treadmill, run outside in a local park or on a trail.

memorable_stories: The Hadza tribe's chair game - when researchers bring chairs, the Hadza people compete for them.
The guest's personal experience of feeling better after exercising in the morning
contrarian_claims: Remote work is unnatural for the human body.
Chairs can have an extremely negative impact on your health.
episode_title: The Diary Of A CEO with Steven Bartlett - The Brain Professor: "Popular Treat Now Considered Deadlier Than Smoking!", "Alcohol Is Shrinking Your Brain" - David Raichlen
